volumeTitle: Amphitryon. The Comedy of Asses. The Pot of Gold. The Two Bacchises. The Captives
label: The Comedy of Asses (Section 2.3.405 - Section 2.4.474b)

sections.label: Section 2.3.405
sections.original: Siquidem hércle Aeacidinis minis animisque expletus incedit ,
sections.translation: By my faith , if , indeed , he were coming filled with the threats and the courage of the grandson ofAeacus [*] , if he were to touch me in his wrath , in his wrath he would be getting a thrashing .

sections.label: Section 2.4.407
sections.original: Quid hoc sít negoti , neminem meum dictum magni facere ?
sections.translation: What ’ s this to do here ? Not a person bares a bit about my orders ! How did I order Libanus to come to the barber ’ s shop , and he didn ’ t come at all . I ’ faith , for sure he hasn ’ t consulted well for his back and his legs .

sections.label: Section 2.4.410
sections.original: Nimis imperiosust .
sections.translation: This is a very overbearing fellow .

sections.label: Section 2.4.410b
sections.original: Vae mihi .
sections.translation: Woe to me this day !

sections.label: Section 2.4.410c
sections.original: Hodie salvere iussi
sections.translation: I bid welcome to Libanus , the freed-man ! Are you set at liberty now ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.411b
sections.original: Obsecro te .
sections.translation: I do implore you .

sections.label: Section 2.4.412
sections.original: Ne tu hercle cum magno malo mihi obviam occessisti .
sections.translation: In good sooth , to your great misfortune surely have you fallen in my way . Why didn ’ t you come to the barber ’ s shop , as I ordered you ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.413b
sections.original: Hic me moratust .
sections.translation: This person detained me .

sections.label: Section 2.4.414
sections.original: Siquidem hércle nunc summum Iovem te dicas detinuisse
sections.translation: I ’ faith , if in fact you were now to say that supreme Jove had detained you ,

sections.label: Section 2.4.415
sections.original: atque is precator adsiet , malam rem effugies numquam .
sections.translation: and he were present to sue for you , you shouldn ’ t escape the evil consequences . Whip-knave , did you disobey my orders ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.416b
sections.original: Perii , hospes .
sections.translation: Stranger , I ’ m done for .

sections.label: Section 2.4.417
sections.original: Quaeso hercle noli , Saurea , mea causa hunc verberare .
sections.translation: Prithee , Saurea , for my sake , don ’ t beat him .

sections.label: Section 2.4.418
sections.original: Vtinam nunc stimulus in manu mihi sit .
sections.translation: I wish , now , I had a whip in my hand —

sections.label: Section 2.4.418b
sections.original: Quiesce quaeso .
sections.translation: Prithee , do be appeased .

sections.label: Section 2.4.419
sections.original: Qui latera conteram tua , quae occalluere plagis .
sections.translation: — With which to lash your sides , which have grown callous with blows .

sections.label: Section 2.4.420
sections.original: abscede ac sine me hunc perdere , qui semper me ira incendit ,
sections.translation: Stand off this way ; let me be the death of this fellow who is continually inflaming me with anger — a thief to whom I can never once enjoin a single thing , but that I must command the same things a hundred times , and din them in his ears [*] . For that reason , now , by my troth , what with bawling and passion , I cannot endure the labour . Have you , you rascal , ( pointing ) ordered this dirt to be removed hence from the door ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.425
sections.original: iussin columnis deici operas araneorum ?
sections.translation: Have you ordered the labours of the spiders to be swept down from the pillars ? Have you ordered those bosses on our door [*] to be brought to brightness ? It ’ s of no use ; I must walk about with a stick , as though I were a lame man . Because only for these single three days I have been giving my constant attendance at the Forum , in order to find some one who requires money upon interest , here ,

sections.label: Section 2.4.430
sections.original: dormitis interea domi , atque erus in hara , haud aedibus , habitat .
sections.translation: in the meantime , are you sleeping at home , and my master is living in a pigstye , not in a house . ( He strikes LIBANUS .) There now , take you that .

sections.label: Section 2.4.431b
sections.original: Hospes , te obsecro , defende .
sections.translation: Prithee , stranger , do take my part .

sections.label: Section 2.4.431c
sections.original: Saurea , oro ,
sections.translation: Saurea , for my sake , I entreat you , do let him go .

sections.label: Section 2.4.432b
sections.original: Eho , ecquis pro vectura olivi
sections.translation: Hark you ! has any one paid for the carriage of that olive oil ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.433b
sections.original: Solvit .
sections.translation: He has paid .

sections.label: Section 2.4.433c
sections.original: Cui datumst ?
sections.translation: To whom was it given ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.433d
sections.original: Sticho vicario ipsi
sections.translation: To Stichus himself , your deputy .

sections.label: Section 2.4.434b
sections.original: Váh , delenire apparas , scio mihi vicarium esse ,
sections.translation: Tut ! you ’ re trying to mollify me [*] . I know that he is my deputy ,

sections.label: Section 2.4.435
sections.original: neque eo esse servom in aedibus eri quí sit pluris quam illest .
sections.translation: and that there isn ’ t a servant in the house who is more valuable to his master than he is . But the wines that I sold yesterday to Exaerambus , the wine merchant , has he yet paid Stichus for them ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.437b
sections.original: Fecisse satis opinor ,
sections.translation: I think he has , in full ; for I saw Exaerambus himself bringing hither his banker .

sections.label: Section 2.4.439
sections.original: Sic dedero . prius quae credidi vix anno post exegi ;
sections.translation: On such terms would I always deal [*] ; before , what I ’ ve trusted , I ’ ve hardly been paid within a year after .

sections.label: Section 2.4.440
sections.original: nunc satagit : adducit domum etiam ultro et scribit nummos .
sections.translation: Now he ’ s quite in a hurry ; even of his own accord he brings him to the house , and writes a transfer of the money [*] . Has Dromo paid down the wages agreed upon ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.441b
sections.original: Dimidio minus opinor .
sections.translation: Less than half , I think .

sections.label: Section 2.4.442
sections.original: Quid relicuom ?
sections.translation: What about the remainder ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.442b
sections.original: Aibat reddere quom extemplo redditum esset ;
sections.translation: He said that he would pay it directly it was paid [*] to him ; for it was retained until he had finished the work that was agreed on to be done by him .

sections.label: Section 2.4.444
sections.original: Scyphos quos utendos dedi Philodamo , rettulitne ?
sections.translation: The cups that I lent to Philodamus , has he brought them back ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.445
sections.original: Non etiam .
sections.translation: Not yet .

sections.label: Section 2.4.445b
sections.original: Hem non ? si velis da ⋯ , commoda homini amico .
sections.translation: What , not yet ? If you wish to make a present [*] , lend to a person that is a friend —

sections.label: Section 2.4.446
sections.original: Perii hercle , iam hic me abegerit suo odio .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I ’ m quite undone ; he ’ ll be just now driving me away with his ill-temper .

sections.label: Section 2.4.446b
sections.original: Heús iam satis tu .
sections.translation: Hallo ! you , enough now . Do you hear what he ’ s saying ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.447b
sections.original: Audio et quiesco .
sections.translation: I hear , and I ’ ll have done .

sections.label: Section 2.4.447c
sections.original: Tandem , opinor ,
sections.translation: At last , I think , he has done : now it ’ s best to accost him before he commences again to prate . ( To LEONIDA .) How soon , sir , will you give me your attention ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.449b
sections.original: Ehem , óptume . quam dudum tu advenisti ?
sections.translation: Oh , by all means — have you been here any time ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.450
sections.original: non hercle te provideram ( quaeso , ne vitio vortas ),
sections.translation: Troth , I didn ’ t observe you ; pray , don ’ t lay it to my charge : anger has so blinded my eyesight .

sections.label: Section 2.4.451b
sections.original: Non mirum factum est .
sections.translation: ’ Tisn ’ t to be wondered at . But if he ’ s at home , I was wanting Demaenetus .

sections.label: Section 2.4.452b
sections.original: Negat esse intus .
sections.translation: Libanus says that he isn ’ t within . But still , if you like to pay that money over to me , I ’ ll give you an acquittance that the account is discharged as to that item .

sections.label: Section 2.4.455
sections.original: Sic potius , ut Demaeneto tibi ero praesente reddam .
sections.translation: This way rather , for me to pay you in the presence of your master , Demaenetus .

sections.label: Section 2.4.456
sections.original: Erus istunc novit atque erum hic .
sections.translation: My master knows him , and he my master .

sections.label: Section 2.4.456b
sections.original: Ero huic praesente reddam .
sections.translation: In his master ’ s presence , I ’ ll pay him .

sections.label: Section 2.4.457
sections.original: Da modo meo periculo , rem salvam ego exhibebo ;
sections.translation: At my peril , so you only pay him , I ’ ll engage the matter ’ s safe . For if our old gentleman were to know that confidence wasn ’ t placed in him , to whom he himself always entrusts the management of all matters , he would be angry .

sections.label: Section 2.4.460
sections.original: Non magni pendo . ne duit , si non volt . sic sine astet .
sections.translation: I don ’ t much care ; don ’ t let him not pay it , if he don ’ t like ; so let him stand here .

sections.label: Section 2.4.461
sections.original: Da , inquam . vah , formido miser , ne hic me tibi arbitretur
sections.translation: Give it him , I say . Oh dear , I ’ m sadly afraid that he ’ ll be thinking that I ’ ve persuaded you not to trust him ; prithee , do give it and don ’ t be afraid . Upon my word , it will be safe .

sections.label: Section 2.4.463b
sections.original: Credam fore , dum quidem ipse in mánu habebo .
sections.translation: I think it will be — so long , indeed , as I myself keep it in my hand . I ’ m a stranger ; I don ’ t know Saurea .

sections.label: Section 2.4.464b
sections.original: At nosce sane .
sections.translation: Well , know him now then .

sections.label: Section 2.4.465
sections.original: Sit , non sit , non edepol scio . si is est , eum esse oportet .
sections.translation: It may be he , it may not be he ; i ’ faith , I know not ; if it ’ s he , why then it must be he . I know for sure that I shall give this up to no person that I don ’ t know .

sections.label: Section 2.4.467
sections.original: Hercle istum di omnes perduint . verbo cave supplicassis .
sections.translation: Troth now , may all the Gods confound the fellow . ( Aloud to LIBANUS .) Take care you don ’ t entreat him with a word . He ’ s arrogant , because he ’ s fingering my twenty minae . No one will take it . ( To the ASS-DEALER .) Take yourself off home , be off from here , and don ’ t be troublesome .

sections.label: Section 2.4.470
sections.original: Nimis iracunde . non decet superbum esse hominem servom .
sections.translation: You are in too angry mood : it isn ’ t right for a person who is a slave to give himself airs .

sections.label: Section 2.4.471
sections.original: Malo hercle iam magno tuo , ni isti nec recte dicis .
sections.translation: By my faith , to your own great misfortune now are you talking uncivilly to him .

sections.label: Section 2.4.472
sections.original: Impure , nihili . non vides irasci ?
sections.translation: Dirty , worthless fellow , don ’ t you see he ’ s angry ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.472b
sections.original: Perge porro .
sections.translation: Be off then .

sections.label: Section 2.4.473
sections.original: Flagitium hominis . da , óbsecro , argentum huic , ne male loquatur .
sections.translation: Scoundrelly fellow . ( Aside to him .) Prithee , do give him the money lest he should abuse you .

sections.label: Section 2.4.474
sections.original: Malum hercle vobis quaeritis .
sections.translation: On my word , you are seeking evil for yourselves .

sections.label: Section 2.4.474b
sections.original: Crura hercle diffringentur ,
sections.translation: By the powers , your legs shall be broken [*] ,
